name: Watermelon
varieties: Charleston Grey; Sugar Babe; Farmers Giant; China Dragon; Emperor Charleston
notes: Seasonal dry-season cash crop; wet-season planting without protection is high risk.

name: Tomato
varieties: Alton; Redland; Melrose; Rio Gold (Cherry Tomato); Rising Sun No. 2 (Hybrid); Summer Taste; Alafua Large
notes: High-management crop; rain and blight pressure can wipe out margins.

name: Eggplant
varieties: Chahat; Round Purple; Pritam / Long Purple; Longhat
notes: Most stable commercial solanaceae crop under Fiji field conditions.

name: Capsicum
varieties: Yolo Wonder B; Yolo Wonder Y; Hybrid Ace; Summer Bell; Blue Star; Yellow Wonder; Golden Bell
notes: Premium but highly sensitive to thrips, virus and BER from irrigation inconsistency.

name: Chillies
varieties: Red Fire; Long Red Cayenne; Birds Eye; Bongo Chilly
notes: High-value volatile crop; thrips, mites, viruses determine profitability.

name: Cucumber
varieties: Greengo; Cascade; Bountiful No. 2; Lissome Swallow; Merry Swallow; Zingpangu; Genuine
notes: Fast turnover crop; regular picking and disease control are non-negotiable.

name: Sweet Corn
varieties: Hawaiian Super Sweet No. 9
notes: Yield depends heavily on stand uniformity and pollination.

name: French Beans
varieties: Contender; Butter Bean; Labrador; Aishwarya
notes: Good rotation crop; keep N modest to preserve nodulation.

name: Long Bean
varieties: Yard Long (Light Green); Yard Long (Dark Green)
notes: Fast-cycle cash crop; highly sensitive to aphids and pod borers; requires trellis.

name: Cabbage
varieties: Green: FS Cross, KK Cross, FY Cross, Grand KK Cross, Summer Autumn, Green Coronet, Baseball; Red varieties available
notes: Best in cooler window; splitting risk rises with erratic irrigation.

name: Squash / Pumpkin
varieties: Queensland Blue; Butternut; Local Selection
notes: Space-demanding vine crop; more forgiving than watermelon.

name: Sweet Potato
varieties: Golden Brown; Local Purple; Kabara; Korolevu Red; Honiara; Talei; Vulatolu; Carrot; Papua; Bua; Vanuavatu
notes: Strong staple; root initiation in first 2-4 weeks determines yield.

name: Cassava
varieties: White Flesh; New Guinea; Turagakula; Nadalei; Vulatolu; Merelesita; Modre; Sokobale; Yabia Damu; Yellow Flesh
notes: Never harvest without market plan; post-harvest deterioration is rapid.

name: Dalo (Taro)
varieties: Hybrid Varieties: Tausala ni Samoa, Wararasa, Samoa Hybrid, Vula Ono, Maleka Dina, Tarova Loa, Tarova ni Viti, Taro Niue
notes: Wetland taro requires steady moisture and leaf blight vigilance.

name: Dalo ni Tana
varieties: Vula (White Flesh); Dravuloa (Red Flesh)
notes: Dryland taro offers flexibility but still needs weed and moisture discipline.

name: Yam
varieties: Early Varieties (All Zones): Lokaloka, Vurai Balavu, Vurai Leka, Yasawa; Late Varieties (All Zones): Beka, Damuni, Filipai White, Filipai Yellow, Futuna, Kivi, Murapoi, Taniela Vula Leka, Veiwa; Late Varieties (Dry Zone): Beka, Damuni, Filipai White, Filipai Yellow, Futuna, Kivi, Murapoi, Niudamu, Taniela Vula Balavu
notes: High labor crop; staking quality strongly affects output.

name: Rourou / Bele
varieties: Local Selection; Round Leaf; Star Leaf
notes: Continuous leafy crop; frequent clean harvest drives value.

name: Ota (Fern)
varieties: Local Selection (wild-harvested; no commercial cultivars currently registered in Fiji)
notes: Shade-loving specialty crop; quality drops fast in dry exposure.

name: Duruka
varieties: Baribari; Duruka Leka; Wainikoro; Thick White; Pilpit Red
notes: High-value local seasonal crop with strong cultural demand.

name: Ginger
varieties: White Ginger (Canton); Red Ginger
notes: Seed quality, drainage and disease control matter more than brute fertilizer.

name: Turmeric
varieties: White; Red; Kiran (Improved)
notes: Strong dried/processed upside if curing is done properly.

name: Kava
varieties: Loa Kasa Leka; Loa Kasa Balavu; Vula Kasa Balavu; Qila Leka; Matakaro Balavu; Yonolulu; Damu; Vula Kasa Leka; Yalu; Qila Balavu; Dokobana Vula; Matakaro Leka; Dokobana Loa
notes: Value depends on age, genetics, shade, drainage and chemical quality.

name: Sugarcane
varieties: Q96; Q117; TC-4; POJ-2878; M-442/51; Local varieties (Non-Commissioned)
notes: Commercial value depends on logistics, sucrose and mill access.

name: Banana / Vudi
varieties: Veimama; Jaina Leka; Lady Finger (Liga ni Marama); Mili
notes: Ratoon-managed crop; wind and leaf disease are key constraints.

name: Pineapple
varieties: Smooth Cayenne; Ripley Queen
notes: Density-sensitive crop; profitability depends on stand uniformity.

name: Papaya
varieties: Sunrise Solo (Fiji Red); Waimanalo
notes: Fast-return fruit crop; virus and sex ratio management matter.

name: Dragon Fruit
varieties: Red Skin with Dark Red Flesh; Red Skin with White Flesh
notes: Needs trellis discipline and targeted market; not a lazy crop.

name: Guava
varieties: Indian Guava; Thailand Guava; Green Pearl
notes: Moderate-input fruit tree with fruit fly pressure risk.

name: Breadfruit
varieties: Uto Dina; Balekana Dina; Balekana ni Samoa; Uto Buco
notes: Traditional long-term food tree; processed value can exceed fresh value.

name: Avocado
varieties: Local Selection (harvest Dec-Apr)
notes: Long-gestation orchard; drainage, root health, wind protection matter most.

name: Cumquat
varieties: Moli (Fijian name for cumquat/kumquat); Nagami (oval fruit); Meiwa (sweet round); local seedling strains
notes: Niche citrus; manageable tree size; specialty-market access required.

name: Coconut
varieties: Fiji Tall; Rotuman Tall; Niu Leka; Niu Magimagi; Niu Drau; Malayan Red Dwarf; Malayan Green Dwarf; Malayan Yellow Dwarf
notes: Long-cycle perennial with multiple value channels: nuts, copra, oil.

name: Goat
varieties: Mixed Anglo Nubian; Boer Goat; Saanen; Toggenburg; Indigenous Breeds
notes: Scalable small ruminant enterprise if grazing and health are controlled.

name: Beef Cattle
varieties: Cross Breeds: Limousine, Hereford, Santa Gertrudis, Angus, French Charolais, Brahman; Pure Breeds: Wagyu, Senepol; Mixed Breed
notes: Land-heavy, slower-turnover livestock system.

name: Dairy Cattle
varieties: Cross Holstein; Friesian; Jersey; Ayrshire; Sahiwal; Brown Swiss
notes: Milk system depends on feed consistency, hygiene and breed performance.

name: Pig
varieties: Large White; Landrace; Duroc; Indigenous Breed
notes: Strong cashflow but feed cost and biosecurity define margin.

name: Broiler Chicken
varieties: Cobb; Ross; Tegal; Indigenous Breed
notes: Fastest turnover; margin is fragile to feed price and mortality.

name: Layer Chicken
varieties: Shavers White; Shavers Brown; Hyline White; Hyline Brown; White Leghorn; Indigenous Breed
notes: Stable cashflow if feed and mortality are tightly managed.

name: Duck
varieties: Muscovy (Cairina moschata); Pekin; Khaki Campbell; Local Fijian strains
notes: More resilient in wet systems than chickens; market is smaller.

name: Tilapia Farm
varieties: Oreochromis niloticus (Nile Tilapia - primary Fiji strain); Red Tilapia (hybrid); Oreochromis mossambicus (Mozambique Tilapia)
notes: Water quality and feed conversion drive returns.

name: Prawn Farm
varieties: Macrobrachium rosenbergii (Giant Freshwater Prawn - primary Fiji species); Penaeus monodon (Black Tiger Prawn)
notes: High-value but technically unforgiving; export-quality handling matters.

name: Napier Grass
varieties: Mott Dwarf Napier; King Grass (Pennisetum x); Bana Grass; Standard Napier (Pennisetum purpureum)
notes: Feed crop; value is internal through livestock conversion efficiency.

name: Legume Cover Crop
varieties: Mucuna pruriens (Velvet Bean); Centrosema pubescens; Pueraria phaseoloides (Tropical Kudzu); Desmodium intortum
notes: Internal soil-building engine; not a direct cash crop.

name: Agarwood
varieties: Aquilaria malaccensis (Agarwood - primary commercial species); Aquilaria crassna; Aquilaria sinensis
notes: Inoculation timing and resin formation determine value more than growth alone.

name: Sandalwood
varieties: Santalum yasi (Fijian Sandalwood - native species); Santalum album (Indian Sandalwood); Santalum austrocaledonicum (New Caledonian)
notes: Host management and heartwood/oil formation are decisive.

name: Pine
varieties: Pinus caribaea (Caribbean Pine - primary Fiji commercial); Pinus elliottii (Slash Pine)
notes: Industrial timber with long rotation and low management after establishment.

name: Mahogany
varieties: Swietenia macrophylla (Honduras / Big-Leaf Mahogany); Swietenia mahagoni (Caribbean Mahogany)
notes: Shoot borer and trunk form matter more than simple survival.

name: Teak
varieties: Tectona grandis (commercial teak - standard Pacific provenance)
notes: Timber value depends on spacing, form and rotation discipline.

name: Apiculture (Honey + Pollination)
varieties: Apis mellifera linguistica (Italian Bees); Apis mellifera mellifera (European Black Bees / Bush Bee)
notes: Dual-output: honey sales plus pollination support across crop blocks.

name: Potato
varieties: Red Pontiac
notes: Same Solanaceae family as tomato. AVOID after solanaceae block. 60-day minimum rest.

name: Cauliflower
varieties: Local Selection; Tropical Sureheart; Snowqueen; White Contessa; Market Wonder; Pioneer
notes: Brassica family; needs cooler conditions; shares clubroot risk with cabbage.

name: Chinese Cabbage
varieties: Pak Choy; Kwang Moon; Wong Bok; Joi Choy
notes: Pak choy, wong bok; fast-cycle brassica; good year-round market.

name: Radish
varieties: Long Whiteside; Awa Cross; Everest; Cagivou; Sitara
notes: Fast-cycle break crop; brassica family restriction applies.

name: Watercress
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Brassicaceae water vegetable; limited to wet/irrigated zones.

name: Onion
varieties: Tropic Red; Texas Early Grano; Superex; Gladallan Brown
notes: Allium family; AVOID after other Alliums; 90-day minimum rest.

name: Garlic
varieties: Local White; Chinese Garlic (Purple Stripe); Shandong White
notes: Allium; allelopathic; high per-kg value; AVOID after onion/spring onion.

name: Spring Onion
varieties: White Lisbon; Yellow Bermuda
notes: Fast-cycle allium; AVOID after garlic/onion block; 90-day rest.

name: Carrot
varieties: Chantenay; Baby Carrot; New Kuroda; New Kuroda Improved
notes: Apiaceae root crop; AVOID after carrot/coriander/parsley; 60-day rest.

name: Lettuce
varieties: Head Type: Great Lakes, Boxhill, Clover; Leafy Type: Green Mignonette, Butter Crunch, Coral Lettuce, Rapid
notes: Fast-cycle leafy; low rotation restriction; excellent break crop.

name: Okra
varieties: Clemson Spineless; Local Long White; Dwarf Long Pod; Local Long Green
notes: Tropical vegetable; heat tolerant; AVOID after other Malvaceae; 60-day rest.

name: Amaranthus
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Local leafy vegetable; excellent low-restriction break crop for all families.

name: Gourd
varieties: Bottle Gourd (Lauki); Snake Gourd (Chichinda); Bitter Gourd (Karela); Ridge Gourd; Sponge Gourd
notes: Cucurbit family; AVOID after watermelon/cucumber/squash; 90-day rest.

name: Zucchini
varieties: Marrow; Black Jack
notes: Cucurbit family; AVOID after watermelon/cucumber/squash; 90-day rest.

name: Maize / Corn
varieties: Nirala
notes: Field grain corn for fodder and staple; different from sweet corn in market use.

name: Rice (Dryland)
varieties: Cagivou; Sitara; Star; Maleka; Totoka; Boldgrain; Uttam; Deepak; Nuinui
notes: Upland rice; Pacific staple grain; lower yield than wetland.

name: Rice (Wetland)
varieties: Star; Maleka; Totoka; Boldgrain; Uttam; Deepak; Nuinui
notes: Paddy rice; flooded system; drain soil before next rotation; rehab required.

name: Sorghum
varieties: Martin Tall
notes: Drought-tolerant grain and fodder; 90-day rest before re-planting grass family.

name: Cowpea
varieties: Tara; Mana
notes: Drought-tolerant nitrogen-fixing legume; excellent break crop and cover.

name: Lablab Bean
varieties: Local Selection (Papdi)
notes: Dual-purpose N-fixer; cover crop and food legume; heavy N loads.

name: Mung Bean
varieties: Samraat
notes: Fast-cycle N-fixer; excellent rotation crop before heavy feeders.

name: Peanut
varieties: Local Spanish
notes: Underground legume; aflatoxin risk post-harvest; high per-kg value if dried well.

name: Pigeon Pea
varieties: Bhapur (Non-Seasonal, Dry Seed); Jaagriti (Seasonal); Kamica (Seasonal)
notes: Long-cycle woody N-fixer; food and fodder use; perennial in some systems.

name: Urd Bean
varieties: Kiran
notes: Black gram; fast-cycle N-fixer; AVOID after French bean/long bean; 60-day rest.

name: Winged Bean
varieties: Early Varieties (All Zones): Lokaloka, Vurai Balavu, Vurai Leka, Yasawa; Late Varieties (All Zones): Beka, Damuni, Filipai White, Filipai Yellow, Futuna, Kivi, Murapoi, Taniela Vula Leka; Late Varieties (Dry Zone): Beka, Damuni, Filipai White, Filipai Yellow, Futuna, Kivi, Murapoi, Niudamu, Taniela Vula Balavu
notes: High-protein tropical legume; all plant parts edible; strong soil N benefit.

name: Kawai / Jicama
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Root legume; N-fixing root crop; limited but growing market.

name: Cardamom
varieties: Malabar; Mysore
notes: Undercanopy spice; shade-tolerant; Zingiberaceae family; 60-day family rest.

name: Coriander
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Fast-cycle herb; Apiaceae family; AVOID after carrot/parsley; 60-day rest.

name: Parsley
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Herb; Apiaceae family; AVOID after carrot/coriander; 60-day rest.

name: Mint
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Perennial spreading herb; low rotation restriction; good ground cover.

name: Herbs (General)
varieties: Chives; Thyme; Borage; Savoury; Lemon Balm; Tarragon; Fennel; Mint; Coriander
notes: Mixed herb block; very low rotation restrictions.

name: Curry Leaves
varieties: Local Selection (Murraya koenigii)
notes: Small perennial Rutaceae tree/shrub; harvest leaves continuously.

name: Plantain
varieties: Qamure; Damuloa; Vudi Dina; Blue Java; Vudi Mami
notes: Cooking banana; managed ratoon system; wind and Sigatoka as for dessert banana.

name: Rockmelon / Cantaloupe
varieties: Yates Rock; Hales PMR 45; Winstons No. 1; Sweet Dream; Summer Dream; Hales Best
notes: High-value cucurbit melon; AVOID after watermelon/cucumber; 90-day rest.

name: Passionfruit
varieties: Local Yellow; Local Purple (self-pollinated)
notes: Vine fruit; Fusarium and woodiness virus are key risks; reblock after 3-4 years.

name: Citrus
varieties: Orange: Late Valencia, Washington Navel; Mandarin: Satsuma, Imperial; Lemon: Eureka; Grapefruit: Wheeny; Lime: Tahitian
notes: Orange, mandarin, lemon, lime; citrus greening (HLB) is key long-term risk.

name: Soursop
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Permanent fruit tree; drought tolerant once established.

name: Kura / Noni
varieties: Local Selection - small fruit varieties preferred
notes: Medicinal fruit tree; drought tolerant; niche health/export market.

name: Drumstick / Moringa
varieties: Local Selection
notes: Multi-purpose tree; leaves and pods harvested; minimal rotation restriction.

name: Mango
varieties: Common Fiji (local), Tommy Atkins, Kensington Pride, Nam Doc Mai, Carabao
notes: Very common in Fiji. Seasonal glut Jan-Feb drops farm gate prices. Value-adding (jam/dried/pulp) doubles margins. Grafted trees produce year 2-3. Seedlings year 5-7.

name: Vanilla
varieties: Tahitian Vanilla (V. tahitensis), Bourbon/Planifolia (V. planifolia)
notes: Year 1-3 establishment = zero revenue. Hand pollination 100% required. Requires shade/support tree. FJD 80-200/kg cured beans farm gate. Export market significant.

name: Cocoa
varieties: Forastero (bulk), Trinitario (flavour), Criollo (premium)
notes: Fiji Cacao & Chocolate Co buys wet beans. Fermentation and drying adds value. Intercrop with banana/coconut for shade. Pod borer and black pod are main risks.

name: Coffee
varieties: Arabica (typica/bourbon), Robusta (limited), Fiji Blue Mountain (local selection)
notes: Highland crop: Taveuni, Namosi, Ba highlands. Arabica quality. Coffee rust (Hemileia) is main threat. Small Fiji specialty market growing.

name: Uronivonu (Bele/Fiji Spinach)
varieties: Wild-type local, Selected large-leaf type
notes: Traditional Fiji leafy green (Abelmoschus manihot type). Cuttings every 2-3 weeks. Extremely hardy. High vitamin/mineral content. Integral to traditional Fijian diet.

name: Sheep (Meat)
varieties: Fiji White, Corriedale Cross, Barbados Blackbelly, Rambouillet Cross
notes: Limited sheep farming in Fiji. Barbados Blackbelly most tick/heat tolerant. Requires rotational grazing. Market demand low vs goat. Worm (nematode) resistance management critical.
